{"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 85, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Torque-to-yield (TTY) bolts reused in a TRD/CC joint", "TTY bolts are single-use per OEM/engineering spec", "Reused TTY bolts have unknown clamp load and may be permanently stretched", "Joint may not maintain specified preload under service load"], "actions": ["Remove and discard all reused torque-to-yield bolts", "Obtain new OEM-specified TTY bolts and any required washers", "Clean and inspect mating surfaces and threads", "Install new fasteners per OEM torque-plus-angle sequence and final yield specification", "Record fastener replacement and torque values on work order"], "rationale": "Torque-to-yield bolts are designed for one-time use; reusing them violates OEM specs and creates a high-risk condition because the bolts may already be yielded, resulting in unpredictable clamp load and potential joint failure.", "references": ["OEM/engineering specs", "Torque-to-yield fastener practice"]}
